item.title: Notes on Linked Lists
item.description: Some notes about working with Linked Lists Types of Linked Lists There are 3 types of linked lists: Singly Doubly Circular Linked lists are in the family of graph data structures. They are graphs of nodes of which there are only one child and no siblings. Uni-directed linked lists, typically known as singly linked lists, have a reference to the next node but not the prev (previous) node. Doubly linked lists are bi-directional with each node having a reference to the child or next node and to the parent or prev node.
